nodes.context: Chinese American youth gang active in San Francisco's Chinatown, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder.
Youth gang formed in Chinatown in 1964 to protect new immigrants; later absorbed into Hop Sing Tong.
Traditional Chinatown organization that absorbed the Wah Ching as its youth wing.
Traditional Chinatown organization rival to Hop Sing Tong; later allied with Joe Boys.
Splinter group from Wah Ching formed in 1969; Joe Fong's group split from it.

edges.context: The Joe Boys and Wah Ching were engaged in a violent war from 1969 to 1977, culminating in the Golden Dragon Massacre.
The infobox lists Suey Sing Tong as allies of the Joe Boys.
The Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong as their youth organization.
The Yau Lai split from the Wah Ching in 1969 due to dissatisfaction with the merger into Hop Sing Tong.
Joe Fong's group splintered from the Yau Lai in early 1971 and later re-absorbed and split again.
The Suey Sing Tong were rivals of the Wah Ching, with violent clashes in 1970.
The Suey Sing Tong and Hop Sing Tong were prominent traditional rival gangs in Chinatown.
